testcase.name: test_cancel_order_twice_does_not_double_restore

failure.message: AssertionError: assert 12 == 10 + where 12 = get_product_stock('prd-test-001') + where get_product_stock = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b296ad0>.get_product_stock + and 'prd-test-001' = <src.products.Product object at 0x10b2969c0>.id

testcase.failure:
self = <test_orders.TestOrderCancellation object at 0x10b23fce0>, order_service = <src.orders.OrdersService object at 0x10b295f20> product_service = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b296ad0>, product = <src.products.Product object at 0x10b2969c0>
def test_cancel_order_twice_does_not_double_restore(self, order_service, product_service, product): """Cancelling an already-cancelled order should not modify stock again.""" initial_stock = product.stock order = order_service.create_order( user_id='usr-001', items=[{'product_id': product.id, 'quantity': 2}], product_service=product_service ) order_service.cancel_order(order.id, product_service) order_service.cancel_order(order.id, product_service) # second call > assert product_service.get_product_stock(product.id) == initial_stock E AssertionError: assert 12 == 10 E + where 12 = get_product_stock('prd-test-001') E + where get_product_stock = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b296ad0>.get_product_stock E + and 'prd-test-001' = <src.products.Product object at 0x10b2969c0>.id demo_suite/tests/test_orders.py:79: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_calculate_total_correct
testcase.failure:
self = <test_orders.TestOrderCalculations object at 0x10b27f110>, order_service = <src.orders.OrdersService object at 0x10b297130> product_service = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b297350>, product = <src.products.Product object at 0x10b297240>
def test_calculate_total_correct(self, order_service, product_service, product): """Total should equal unit_price * quantity with decimal precision.""" # product.unit_price = 999.99, quantity = 2 → expected 1999.98 order = order_service.create_order( user_id='usr-001', items=[{'product_id': product.id, 'quantity': 2}], product_service=product_service ) total = order_service.calculate_total(order.id, product_service) > assert total == pytest.approx(1999.98) E assert 1999 == 1999.98 ± 0.00199998 E E comparison failed E Obtained: 1999 E Expected: 1999.98 ± 0.00199998
demo_suite/tests/test_orders.py:93: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_create_product_success

failure.message: TypeError: can only concatenate str (not "UUID") to str

testcase.failure:
self = <test_products.TestProductCreation object at 0x10b27f610> empty_product_service = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b2bbc50>
def test_create_product_success(self, empty_product_service): """Products can be created with valid data.""" > product = empty_product_service.create_product( name='Laptop', type='electronics', stock=10, unit_price=999.99 )
demo_suite/tests/test_products.py:9: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b2bbc50>, name = 'Laptop', type = 'electronics', stock = 10, unit_price = 999.99
def create_product(self, name, type, stock, unit_price): new_product = Product( > id='prd-'+ uuid.uuid4(), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ name=name, type=type, stock=stock, unit_price=unit_price ) E TypeError: can only concatenate str (not "UUID") to str demo_suite/src/products.py:23: TypeError

failure.message: TypeError: can only concatenate str (not "UUID") to str

self = <test_products.TestProductCreation object at 0x10b27f750> empty_product_service = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b3689b0>

testcase.name: test_update_stock_to_zero

failure.message: AssertionError: assert 10 == 0 + where 10 = get_product_stock('prd-test-001') + where get_product_stock = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b19abd0>.get_product_stock + and 'prd-test-001' = <src.products.Product object at 0x10b33f690>.id

testcase.failure:
self = <test_products.TestProductUpdate object at 0x10b27fc50> product_service = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b19abd0>, product = <src.products.Product object at 0x10b33f690>
def test_update_stock_to_zero(self, product_service, product): """Setting stock to 0 should reflect as 0, not retain the old value.""" product_service.update_product(product.id, stock=0) > assert product_service.get_product_stock(product.id) == 0 E AssertionError: assert 10 == 0 E + where 10 = get_product_stock('prd-test-001') E + where get_product_stock = <src.products.ProductService object at 0x10b19abd0>.get_product_stock E + and 'prd-test-001' = <src.products.Product object at 0x10b33f690>.id demo_suite/tests/test_products.py:55: AssertionError

self = <test_users.TestUserRegistration object at 0x10b324c30>, user_service = <src.users.UserService object at 0x10b341950>
def test_register_duplicate_email_raises(self, user_service): """Two accounts should not share the same email address.""" user_service.register('carol', 'carol@example.com', 'pass1') > with pytest.raises(ValueError): ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'ValueError'>
demo_suite/tests/test_users.py:22: Failed

testcase.name: test_authenticate_is_case_sensitive

failure.message: AssertionError: assert True is False + where True = authenticate('grace', 'secret') + where authenticate = <src.users.UserService object at 0x10b3a49e0>.authenticate

testcase.failure:
self = <test_users.TestAuthentication object at 0x10b324fc0>, user_service = <src.users.UserService object at 0x10b3a49e0>
def test_authenticate_is_case_sensitive(self, user_service): """Authentication should be case-sensitive — 'Secret' != 'secret'.""" user_service.register('grace', 'grace@example.com', 'Secret') > assert user_service.authenticate('grace', 'secret') is False E AssertionError: assert True is False E + where True = authenticate('grace', 'secret') E + where authenticate = <src.users.UserService object at 0x10b3a49e0>.authenticate
demo_suite/tests/test_users.py:52: AssertionError
